questions.question: What was CLIP's core bet about supervision?
options: More human labelers produce better labels
The web's billion+ image-caption pairs carry free supervisory signal — treat matching as the task: each image must match its own caption against N-1 in-batch distractors, no class labels needed
Synthetic captions beat real ones
Vision models need no text at all
questions.explanation: 400M noisy pairs and a contrastive loss replaced expensive labeled datasets — and the resulting embedding space does zero-shot classification it was never explicitly trained for.

questions.question: How is CLIP's similarity computed between an image and a caption?
options: Edit distance between pixels and characters
Both towers output unit-normalized D-dim vectors, so cosine similarity is just the dot product f(x)ᵀg(y), scaled by a learned temperature
A third model scores each pair
Pixel-wise MSE against a rendered caption
questions.explanation: Dual encoders (ViT/ResNet image tower, small transformer text tower) project into one space; the batch similarity matrix S[i,j] = cos(f(xᵢ), g(yⱼ))/τ feeds the contrastive loss.

questions.question: What does InfoNCE actually optimize in a batch of N pairs?
options: Reconstruction error of the image
Symmetric cross-entropy over the similarity matrix — each image must beat N-1 distractor captions and vice versa, so bigger batches mean more negatives and stronger signal (CLIP trained at batch 32k)
The temperature parameter alone
The norm of the embeddings
questions.explanation: loss = (CE over rows + CE over columns)/2 with identity labels. In-batch negatives are the engine, which is exactly why batch size is one of the four pretraining levers.

questions.question: Why does SigLIP's sigmoid loss scale better than CLIP's softmax?
options: Sigmoid is more accurate mathematically

Softmax needs the full similarity matrix synchronized — all-gather of every embedding to every replica, quadratic communication in world size; sigmoid treats each pair as independent binary classification, no global sync
Sigmoid uses half-precision automatically
Softmax cannot run on TPUs
questions.explanation: Element-wise pairwise loss (diagonal = positive, rest = negative) removes the distributed-softmax bottleneck, letting SigLIP scale past batch 32k at lower cost — why it became the dominant 2026 vision tower.

questions.question: How does zero-shot ImageNet classification work with CLIP?
options: Fine-tune a classification head on ImageNet first
Embed text templates like "a photo of a {class}" for every class, embed the image, take argmax over cosine similarities — no ImageNet training at all
Ask the text encoder to generate the label
Nearest-neighbor lookup in the training set
questions.explanation: "A photo of a cat" embeds near cat images that were never labeled 'cat'. Prompt template quality is one of the four levers (with batch size, temperature, and data quality).
